name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Los Angeles, Ca
description_short: NACA is a nonprofit mortgage lender offering character-based mortgages with no down payment, no closing costs, and below-market rates to underserved communities.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years challenging predatory lending practices and advocating for economic justice. The organization originated from the first-ever housing trust fund negotiation and has evolved into a major force in affordable homeownership. NACA serves as both a lender and housing counselor, providing character-based mortgage products that prioritize borrower circumstances over credit scores.

NACA's flagship mortgage product offers some of the most aggressive terms in the industry: zero down payment required, zero closing costs or fees, no mortgage insurance, no credit score consideration, and fixed interest rates below market (5.625% for 30-year as of April 2026). They also provide HUD-approved housing counseling, volunteer opportunities through their NACtivist program, and advocacy against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors. The organization hosts "Achieve the Dream" events across the country where prospective members can access services and become NACA Qualified.

What distinguishes NACA is their explicit mission to close the racial wealth disparity gap through lending practices that evaluate borrower character rather than credit metrics. They have helped 500,000 homeowners achieve ownership, originated 75,000 NACA mortgages, and maintain $20 billion in mortgage commitments. They serve 3 million people and provide 30% of all HUD housing counseling nationally. This approach fundamentally differs from traditional mortgage lenders who rely heavily on credit scores and require substantial down payments.

However, NACA's business model requires active member participation and engagement. Membership appears to involve fees (they mention "priority membership"), and becoming "NACA Qualified" requires attending multi-day Achieve the Dream events. The website does not specify eligibility requirements, approval timelines, or geographic service limitations. Their rates, while below-market, still represent fixed mortgages and are therefore subject to interest rate fluctuations.
pros: Zero down payment requirement — eliminates the largest barrier to homeownership for low-income borrowers
No closing costs or mortgage insurance — removes $3,000-$10,000+ in typical upfront expenses
Character-based lending ignores credit scores — serves borrowers with poor or no credit history
Below-market fixed rates (5.625% 30-yr, 5.125% 20-yr, 5% 15-yr as of April 2026) — competitive pricing
Provides HUD-approved housing counseling — delivers financial education alongside lending
500,000+ homeowners helped and 75,000 mortgages originated — established track record and scale
Advocacy against predatory landlords and corporate investors — mission-aligned social justice work
cons: Requires attendance at multi-day Achieve the Dream events to qualify — time commitment barrier for working families
Website does not disclose membership fees, eligibility criteria, or approval timelines — limited transparency
Geographic service appears limited to specific event locations (Little Rock, Milwaukee, Fort Myers, Hartford, St. Louis mentioned) — not nationwide availability
No information about debt-to-income limits, income requirements, or maximum loan amounts — borrowers cannot self-assess eligibility
Character-based underwriting criteria not defined on website — approval standards unclear
best_for: Low-to-moderate income borrowers with poor or no credit history who cannot qualify for traditional mortgages
First-time homebuyers in underserved communities, particularly people of color facing systemic lending discrimination
Families who cannot save for a down payment but have stable employment and character references
Borrowers seeking both a mortgage product and financial education/housing counseling
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-to-moderate income first-time homebuyers, particularly people of color, who lack down payment savings or have damaged credit but maintain stable employment and character references. The main caveat is that qualifying requires attending a multi-day in-person event in one of their limited service areas, and membership involves undisclosed fees—making this a more participatory, mission-driven alternative to traditional mortgage lenders rather than a convenience-based option.
services: Character-based mortgage origination with no credit score consideration
Zero down payment mortgages with fixed interest rates
Mortgages with no closing costs or mortgage insurance requirements
HUD-approved housing counseling and financial education
Multi-day Achieve the Dream events for member qualification
Member portal for document upload and application status tracking
NACtivist volunteer program and community organizing opportunities
Lender, servicer, and real estate professional partnerships
Advocacy and campaign support against predatory landlords
Podcast series (Breaking Barriers, NACA Live American Dream Program) on homeownership and economic justice
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
